mechanism_narrative: A low federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, increasing present equity values and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC is below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during low FEDFUNDS periods do not exceed baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate below 6.5 percent signals a tightening labor market that boosts wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50 percent of instances when UNRATE is below 6.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Joint growth in retail sales and subdued inflation indicates healthy real demand without imminent monetary tightening pressure, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term of RSAFS and CPIAUCSL adds no incremental predictive power over RSAFS alone in a 3-year rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, a rising federal funds rate combined with accelerating consumer price inflation increases discount rates and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55 percent of observations during triggered tightening periods.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output and rising corporate revenues, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are not significantly positive during months when INDPRO increases (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts sustained above 950 thousand units signal durable construction momentum and broader household formation, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are negative in more than 45 percent of months when HOUST exceeds 950 thousand units.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate provide a confirming signal of labor market strength, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Directional accuracy of the joint PAYEMS-UNRATE signal does not exceed that of PAYEMS alone over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month consumer price inflation exceeding 0.25 percent erodes real corporate earnings expectations and elevates the risk of Federal Reserve tightening, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 20-day returns in more than 60 percent of instances when month-over-month CPIAUCSL exceeds 0.25 percent.
